skill.name: googlesheets-automation
skill.description: Automate Google Sheets workflows through Rube MCP, including reads, writes, appends, upserts, formatting, filters, and spreadsheet setup.
skill.summary: Guides AI agents through Google Sheets automation using Rube MCP and Composio tools.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backtick-formatted names as Ruby or shell execution, but SKILL.md is documentation and the backticks mark MCP tool names, parameters, and examples. The only confirmed risk is use of an external Rube MCP endpoint and Google OAuth flow, which is expected for this Google Sheets automation skill.

low_findings.title: External MCP Service Required
low_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server and authenticate a Google Sheets connection. This is expected functionality, but it sends spreadsheet operations through a third-party MCP provider and should be disclosed to users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network endpoint and OAuth connection flow are explicitly documented. This is a real external-service dependency, but it aligns with the stated purpose of the skill.

low_findings.title: Backtick Tool Names Are Static-Analysis False Positives
low_findings.description: The many external command findings refer to Markdown inline code for Rube MCP tool names and parameter names, not executable Ruby or shell backtick syntax. No evidence of local command execution was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation and the cited lines are prerequisites, tool sequences, or quick-reference rows. There is no script block or instruction to execute local shell commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: The high weak-cryptography finding at line 3 does not correspond to cryptographic code. The line is a plain description of Google Sheets read and write capabilities.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains metadata text, not hashing, encryption, or credential handling logic. No cryptographic operation appears in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings at line 94 refer to a Google Sheets filter.range parameter. No host, process, environment, or filesystem reconnaissance behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line describes a spreadsheet grid range for filtering. It is unrelated to system discovery or local machine inspection.

content.value_statement: Manual spreadsheet updates waste time and increase data-entry mistakes. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured Google Sheets actions using Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Find spreadsheets by name when the spreadsheet ID is unknown.
Read bounded Google Sheets ranges and handle missing empty values.
Write, update, and append rows using the correct two-dimensional value format.
Create spreadsheets, add tabs, rename tabs, hide tabs, and inspect spreadsheet metadata.
Search rows, apply basic filters, and upsert records by a unique key column.
Apply cell formatting with numeric worksheet IDs and Google Sheets color values.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Google Sheets OAuth connection.
Does not bypass Google account permissions or sharing restrictions.
Large unbounded ranges can time out and should be avoided.
Destructive actions such as deleting rows or columns still require careful user confirmation.

use_cases.title: Update Sales Operations Sheets
use_cases.description: Append new leads, upsert account records, and format status columns in shared pipeline spreadsheets.

use_cases.title: Prepare Reporting Workbooks
use_cases.description: Create report tabs, read source ranges, apply filters, and write cleaned results to bounded ranges.

use_cases.title: Maintain Inventory Trackers
use_cases.description: Find product rows by SKU, update stock counts, and add new products without duplicating records.

prompt_templates.title: Read a Sheet Range
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Google Sheets automation skill to read Sheet1!A1:F100 from my spreadsheet. First confirm the Rube tools and available sheet tabs.

prompt_templates.title: Append New Rows
prompt_templates.prompt: Add these new customer rows to the Customers tab. Use append mode, keep values as user-entered data, and report the updated range.

prompt_templates.title: Upsert Records by Key
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the Inventory tab by SKU. Match on the SKU header, update existing rows, append missing rows, and use strict column matching.

prompt_templates.title: Build and Format a Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a monthly report tab, write the summary table, freeze the header row, apply bold formatting, and verify the final sheet metadata.

output_examples.input: Read the first 100 rows from the Leads tab and summarize missing email addresses.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms the spreadsheet and tab, reads the bounded range, then reports which rows have missing email values.

output_examples.input: Add three new orders to the Orders tab and tell me where they were placed.
output_examples.output: The assistant appends the rows, verifies the returned updated range, and summarizes the number of rows added.

output_examples.input: Format the header row on the Budget tab with bold text and a green background.
output_examples.output: The assistant resolves the numeric worksheet ID, applies formatting to the header range, and suggests a readback check.

best_practices: Call the Rube tool search first so tool schemas and parameter names are current.
Use bounded A1 ranges such as A1:Z10000 to reduce timeout risk.
Read the existing sheet layout before writing or upserting data.
anti_patterns: Do not use unbounded ranges on large spreadsheets unless the user accepts slower operations.
Do not assume the default tab is named Sheet1 across all locales.
Do not delete rows or columns without confirming exact bounds first.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Google account?
faq.answer: Yes. Users need an active Google Sheets connection through Rube MCP and Google OAuth.

faq.question: Can it create new spreadsheets?
faq.answer: Yes. The guide includes spreadsheet creation and tab management workflows.

faq.question: Can it update existing rows instead of only appending?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes upserting rows by a unique key column such as Email or SKU.

faq.question: Can it format cells?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers header formatting, colors, font settings, and required worksheet IDs.

faq.question: Does it run local shell commands?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed file is Markdown documentation for MCP tool calls.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: Spreadsheet operations depend on a third-party MCP endpoint and the permissions granted through Google OAuth.
